$comment: Harness scope surface (note 9). The foundational data structure: scheduler picks the in_progress item, verifier gates active->passing, handoff reads state. JSON (not MD) so it isn't casually overwritten. Every feature starts failing; it must EARN 'passing' via its verification command + recorded evidence (commit hash). Hooks: check-wip.py (exactly one in_progress), check-evidence.py (no 'passing' without evidence).
rules: One feature in_progress at a time. active->passing ONLY when `verification` runs green AND `evidence` records the proof (commit hash + verify reference). Do not hand-edit a feature to 'passing'.

features.behavior: Atomic fan-in decrement guarded in-transaction (BOM 03-B4, the highest-weight state-across-boundaries mechanism): complete_task_and_decrement(session, job_id, task_id, audio_key)->int|None in core/infra/queries.py does, in ONE transaction, a durable idempotent claim (UPDATE tasks SET status='DONE', audio_key WHERE task_id AND status<>'DONE') then, ONLY if rowcount==1, an atomic barrier decrement (UPDATE jobs SET pending_count=pending_count-1 RETURNING pending_count). features.behavior: Set pending_count only on the first CAS out of PENDING (BOM backlog-H15, parse re-runnability corollary of H2): begin_parse(session, job_id, n_blocks)->bool does a CAS UPDATE jobs SET status=PARSING, pending_count=N WHERE job_id AND status=PENDING. rowcount==1 => first run (counter set, returns True); rowcount==0 => parse redelivery (job already advanced, counter untouched, returns False). A parse re-run MUST NOT reset pending_count (that would resurrect already-decremented tasks and the job would never finish). NOTE: FSM legal successor of PENDING is PARSING (state.py LEGAL), not GENERATING as the card title loosely states.

features.behavior: Crash recovery: manual ack LAST (after Postgres commit), prefetch set, NACK->retry path. docker kill mid-job loses no message.
verification: make e2e -k crash_recovery (docker kill worker mid-job -> redelivered, job converges)
features.evidence: uv run pytest -m e2e -k crash_recovery -> 1 passed in 42.78s (test_job_survives_worker_crash_and_completes_on_recovery) against the LIVE compose stack. DETERMINISTIC design: the sim's only vendor latency is asyncio.sleep(0) so a job processes in ms — a mid-handler kill cannot be timed without racing. Instead: docker kill WORKER first, POST a 5-block job, assert it stays PENDING across a 5s outage (JobCreated sits durably in q.parse, no consumer, no loss), docker start WORKER, then assert COMPLETED + final_key set + pending_count==0 within 300s (worker re-runs its uv-sync entrypoint ~30s, reconnects, runs full parse->tts->stitch). A lost message would strand the job in PENDING forever, so reaching COMPLETED IS the no-loss proof. The complementary in-flight unacked-redelivery path (ack-last) is L3-proven by the idempotency handlers (B4 conditional claim, W4 H-EMIT, W5 stitch H5); split documented in DECISIONS Phase-6 R3.1. commit 700e587 base (uncommitted; user handles git).
